title: gradient-of-gradient with VECTOR param silently returns zeros (docs claim the 1-d form works)
goal: (gradient (lambda (v) (vref (gradient (lambda (w) (* (vref w 0) (vref w 0) (vref w 0))) v) 0)) (vector 2.0)) returns #(0), expected #(12); the 2-d form returns #(0 0), expected #(8 6). The scalar-point form of the same second derivative is CORRECT (returns 12), and docs/breakdown/AUTODIFF.md 'Higher-Order Derivatives' documents the 1-d vector form as returning #(12.0). Same silent-zero family as ESH-0093 (vector gradient over inner derivative) and ESH-0078 (named-fn inner gradient) but a distinct cell: both levels are reverse-mode vector gradients.
fix_hint: The forward-4-jet/__ad_pert_level nested path (from #84/#95) only fires for the scalar-point gradient form; the vector-param outer gradient falls back to the old reverse-over-reverse path whose inner tape sees the outer AD variables as constants. Extend the nested-level handling to vector-point gradients (per-component seeding), or route the outer vector gradient through the same jet machinery per component.
acceptance: AD-oracle cells nest.gofg.a.v1/nest.gofg.b.v2 flip XKNOWN->PASS on -r AND AOT
docs example in AUTODIFF.md 'Higher-Order Derivatives' actually returns #(12.0)
campaign_payoff: Closes the last silent-zero nested-AD family cell; second-order optimization on vector params (natural for ML) currently returns all-zero curvature with no error.
